summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Two-Party Proof Comparison demo. Backend: 12/12 pytest cases passed covering normalization equivalence (user_id case/whitespace, amount formats 100.5/100.50/'100.5'), differentiation (different amount/user_id produce different hash), determinism (same input -> same SHA-256), validation (400 on invalid/empty amount, 400 on invalid timestamp, 422 on missing field), and ISO-UTC timestamp normalization with .000Z. Frontend: full Playwright e2e across all 12 target flows passed — identical pre-filled A/B records, independent proof generation with Proof Ready badges, compare-btn disabled-until-both-proofs, green match result, proof invalidation + stale badge + result clearing + compare disabled on edit, regenerate -> mismatch flow, tech-details drawer collapsed by default + expands with proof_hash/normalized_payload/algorithm/canonicalization, and reset-demo-btn restores defaults.
critical_code_review_comments: demo_routes.py uses datetime.utcnow() (deprecated in Python 3.12+). Prefer datetime.now(timezone.utc) — non-blocking but a future-proofing nit.

canonicalize.normalize_value converts whole-number floats to int (e.g. 100.0 -> 100). Not triggered in demo (amount is pre-stringified to '100.50'), so no behavioural impact, but worth noting for future field additions.

_normalize_timestamp_safe: normalize_timestamp returns the raw string on parse failure; the safe wrapper correctly detects this by re-parsing. Works as designed — just slightly indirect. Could be clearer if normalize_timestamp raised on failure.
Frontend staleness is computed by JSON.stringify(record) snapshot comparison — reliable for this flat shape; fine as-is.
seed_data_creation: None — stateless endpoint, no DB writes
context_for_next_testing_agent: Demo feature is fully working end-to-end. Backend normalization rules verified: user_id is trim+lowercase, transaction_id is trim-only (opaque), amount is trim+comma-strip+2-decimal, timestamp is ISO UTC with .000Z. Frontend data-testids are all present and stable. If revisiting, re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_demo_proof.py. Existing /api/fea and /api/public routes intentionally no longer surfaced in UI — per spec, do not flag.
